name: Pasulj (Serbian Bean Soup)
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Dried white beans (navy or cannellini)
recipeInstructions:
1. Pick over the beans for bits of grit and chaff, and rinse them twice in cold water.
2. Place the beans in a large saucepan and cover with water to a depth of 1 to 2 inches. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer gently for half an hour.
3. Remove pan from heat and add enough cold water to cover beans to a depth of 3 inches. Let settle for a minute or two, then pour off the water and replace with fresh water. Return to a boil and reduce heat to simmer.
4. Add onions, garlic, bay leaves, parsley, tomato paste, carrot, and peppercorns to beans. Cook gently over very low heat for 1 1/2 to 2 hours until beans are soft.
5. After 45 minutes of cooking, add kielbasa.
6. Fifteen minutes before serving, add salt and prepare liaison: Heat oil until smoking lightly; stir in paprika, followed by flour. Mix to a thin paste.
7. Add liaison to soup, stirring well. Simmer for 5 minutes until thick and rich.
8. Serve with bread and good red wine. Serves 5-6. Time: 2-2.5 hours
